definition: Formed or devised a plan or idea in the mind
example: Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth on this continent a new nation, conceived in liberty.

definition: A statement or assertion that expresses a judgment or opinion
example: Dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal.

definition: Suffer something difficult or unpleasant patiently
example: Now we are engaged in a great civil war, testing whether that nation can long endure.

definition: Honor as holy; make holy; consecrate
example: We have come to dedicate a portion of that field as a final resting place for those who here gave their lives that that nation might live.

definition: Take away; diminish the value of
example: The world will little note, nor long remember what we say here, but it can never forget what they did here.

definition: Not finished; incomplete
example: It is for us the living, rather, to be dedicated here to the unfinished work which they who fought here have thus far so nobly advanced.

definition: In a noble manner; with nobility
example: They who fought here have thus far so nobly advanced.

definition: Decide firmly on a course of action
example: That we here highly resolve that these dead shall not have died in vain.

definition: Producing no result; useless
example: That these dead shall not have died in vain.

definition: Suffer death, typically in a violent, sudden, or untimely way
example: That government of the people, by the people, for the people, shall not perish from the earth.

definition: The fact or process of being set free from legal, social, or political restrictions
example: The emancipation of the slave was the great cause of his presidency.

definition: The action or fact of joining together
example: A house divided against itself cannot stand; this Union must be preserved.

definition: Separated into parts; having a separate opinion or aim
example: A house divided against itself cannot stand.

definition: The state of being free within society from oppressive restrictions
example: Conceived in liberty and dedicated to the proposition that all men are created equal.

definition: The state of being equal, especially in status, rights, or opportunities
example: All men are created equal.

definition: The intention or desire to do evil; ill will
example: With malice toward none, with charity for all.

definition: The voluntary giving of help to those in need
example: With malice toward none, with charity for all.

definition: The quality of being steady, secure, or fixed; resoluteness
example: Let us have faith that right makes might, and in that faith, let us, to the end, dare to do our duty as we understand it.

definition: The quality of being morally right or justifiable
example: Let us have faith that right makes might.

definition: Property that is or may be inherited; an inheritance
example: Our fathers brought forth a new nation that is our common heritage.

definition: The act of abolishing; an annulling; abrogation
example: the abolition of debts; the abolition of laws; the abolition of slavery; the abolition of police;; the abolition of taxes

definition: A person who advocates the political interests of working farmers
example: The speech returned to the idea of agrarian as a guiding principle.

definition: Self-government; freedom to act or function independently.
example: The speech returned to the idea of autonomy as a guiding principle.

definition: The state of being enslaved or the practice of slavery.
example: In Judeo-Christian tradition, the Israelites fled bondage at the hands of the Egyptians, only to wander in the wilderness for the next four decades.

definition: The status of being a citizen, in its various senses.
example: The speech returned to the idea of citizenship as a guiding principle.

definition: The exchange or buying and selling of commodities; especially the exchange of merchandise, on a large scale, between different places or communities; extended trade or traffic.
example: The speech returned to the idea of commerce as a guiding principle.

definition: The process of bringing peace and harmony; the ending of strife.
example: The speech returned to the idea of conciliation as a guiding principle.

definition: A walk that is taken regularly for good health and wellbeing.
example: The speech returned to the idea of constitutional as a guiding principle.

definition: Disagreement with the ideas, doctrines, decrees, etc. of a political party, government or religion.
example: The speech returned to the idea of dissent as a guiding principle.

definition: The right to vote at a public election or referendum; see: suffrage, suffragette.
example: The speech returned to the idea of franchise as a guiding principle.

definition: The part of a country which borders or faces another country or unsettled region
example: The speech returned to the idea of frontier as a guiding principle.

definition: Something which causes grief.
example: The speech returned to the idea of grievance as a guiding principle.

definition: A house together with surrounding land and buildings, especially on a farm; the property comprising these.
example: The speech returned to the idea of homestead as a guiding principle.

definition: The tendency to work persistently. Diligence.
example: Over the years, their industry and business sense made them wealthy.

definition: Effort expended on a particular task; toil, work.
example: The speech returned to the idea of labor as a guiding principle.

definition: A means of providing the necessities of life for oneself (for example, a job or income).
example: an independent livelihood; to make / earn / gain a good livelihood

definition: A public declaration of principles, policies, or intentions, especially that of a political party.
example: A creed is a manifesto of religious or spiritual beliefs.

definition: The state or quality of being moderate; avoidance of extremes
example: The speech returned to the idea of moderation as a guiding principle.

definition: A local law
example: The speech returned to the idea of ordinance as a guiding principle.

definition: A formal, written request made to an official person or organized body, often containing many signatures.
example: The speech returned to the idea of petition as a guiding principle.

definition: The quality or state of being plural, or in the plural number.
example: The speech returned to the idea of pluralism as a guiding principle.

definition: A political approach that claims to represent the interests of ordinary people
example: His brand of populism turned economic anger into political energy.

definition: All the future generations, especially the descendants of a specific person.
example: The speech returned to the idea of posterity as a guiding principle.

definition: A statement which is proclaimed; formal public announcement.
example: The speech returned to the idea of proclamation as a guiding principle.

definition: The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune
example: The speech returned to the idea of prosperity as a guiding principle.

definition: A thing that has been reconstructed or restored to an earlier state.
example: The speech returned to the idea of reconstruction as a guiding principle.

definition: A direct popular vote on a proposed law or constitutional amendment. The adposition on is usually used before the related subject of the vote.
example: The speech returned to the idea of referendum as a guiding principle.

definition: That which represents something else.
example: The Venus of Willendorf was an early representation of the female body.

definition: One who reforms, or who works for reform.
example: The speech returned to the idea of reformer as a guiding principle.

definition: The right or chance to vote, express an opinion, or participate in a decision.
example: The speech returned to the idea of suffrage as a guiding principle.

definition: A system of government-imposed duties levied on imported or exported goods; a list of such duties, or the duties themselves.
example: The speech returned to the idea of tariff as a guiding principle.

definition: A building that is rented to multiple tenants, especially a low-rent, run-down one.
example: The speech returned to the idea of tenement as a guiding principle.

definition: Labour, work, especially of a grueling nature.
example: The speech returned to the idea of toil as a guiding principle.

definition: The act or result of being uplifted.
example: The speech returned to the idea of uplift as a guiding principle.

definition: Health, safety, happiness and prosperity; well-being in any respect.
example: The speech returned to the idea of welfare as a guiding principle.

definition: The well-being of a community.
example: The speech returned to the idea of commonwealth as a guiding principle.

definition: Act of joining together into a single political entity.
example: It is 106 years since federation.

definition: The state of being accountable; liability to be called on to render an account; accountableness; responsible for; answerable for.
example: The speech returned to the idea of accountability as a guiding principle.

definition: Someone whose job is to speak for someone's case in a court of law; a counsel.
example: The speech returned to the idea of advocate as a guiding principle.

definition: The state of being allied.
example: matrimonial alliances; an alliance between church and state, or between two countries

definition: To render larger, more extended, or more intense.
example: amplify a microscopes

definition: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit
example: The speaker tried to adapt the audience with a calm but forceful appeal.

definition: A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
example: The speech returned to the idea of balance as a guiding principle.

definition: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed in the process.
example: The speech returned to the idea of catalyst as a guiding principle.

definition: A confrontation; a dare.
example: The speech returned to the idea of challenge as a guiding principle.

definition: A being involved in the action of a story.
example: The speech returned to the idea of character as a guiding principle.

definition: An option; a decision; an opportunity to choose or select something.
example: Do I have a choice of what color to paint it?

definition: The state or measure of being clear, either in appearance, thought or style; lucidity.
example: Lack of clarity on the part of the teacher will cause confusion among the students.

definition: A temporary group or union of organizations, usually formed for a particular advantage.
example: The Liberal Democrats and Conservative parties formed a coalition government in 2010.

definition: A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.
example: The speech returned to the idea of community as a guiding principle.

definition: A process of decision-making that seeks widespread agreement among group members.
example: The speech returned to the idea of consensus as a guiding principle.

definition: A firmly held belief.
example: The speech returned to the idea of conviction as a guiding principle.

definition: The quality of being confident, not afraid or easily intimidated, but without being incautious or inconsiderate.
example: It takes a lot of courage to be successful in business.

definition: Believable or plausible.
example: Her credible tone gave the address an unmistakable sense of purpose.

definition: An argument, or discussion, usually in an ordered or formal setting, often with more than two people, generally ending with a vote or other decision.
example: After a four-hour debate, the committee voted to table the motion.

definition: A conversation or other form of discourse between two or more individuals.
example: Bill and Melinda maintained a dialogue via email over the course of their long-distance relationship.

definition: The state of being dignified or worthy of esteem: elevation of mind or character.
example: The speech returned to the idea of dignity as a guiding principle.

definition: Verbal exchange, conversation.
example: The speech returned to the idea of discourse as a guiding principle.

definition: Identification with or understanding of the thoughts, feelings, or emotional state of another person.
example: She had a lot of empathy for her neighbor; she knew what it was like to lose a parent too.

definition: A sincere attempt; a determined or assiduous effort towards a specific goal; assiduous or persistent activity.
example: The speech returned to the idea of endeavor as a guiding principle.

definition: Fairness, impartiality, or justice as determined in light of 'natural law' or 'natural right'.
example: The speech returned to the idea of equity as a guiding principle.

definition: The study of principles relating to right and wrong conduct.
example: The speech returned to the idea of ethics as a guiding principle.

definition: Facts or observations presented in support of an assertion.
example: There is no evidence that anyone was here earlier.

definition: The property of being fair or equitable.
example: Some questioned the fairness of the new laws.

definition: Mental or emotional strength that enables courage in the face of adversity.
example: The speech returned to the idea of fortitude as a guiding principle.

definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think without unjust restraint
example: The speech framed freedom as the birthright of every citizen.

definition: The time ahead; those moments yet to be experienced.
example: The speech returned to the idea of future as a guiding principle.

definition: The act of governing
example: The speech returned to the idea of govern as a guiding principle.

definition: Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation.
example: The guide led us around the museum and explained the exhibits.

definition: Agreement or accord.
example: The speech returned to the idea of harmony as a guiding principle.

definition: A spell or ability that restores hit points or removes a status ailment.
example: The speech returned to the idea of heal as a guiding principle.

definition: The characteristic of being humble; humbleness in character and behavior.
example: The speech returned to the idea of humility as a guiding principle.

definition: The striking of one body against another; collision.
example: The speech returned to the idea of impact as a guiding principle.

definition: An addition or annex to a group, set, or total.
example: The poem was a new inclusion in the textbook.

definition: The power to affect, control or manipulate something or someone; the ability to change the development of fluctuating things such as conduct, thoughts or decisions.
example: I have absolutely no influence over him.

definition: To infuse into the mind; to communicate to the spirit; to convey, as by a divine or supernatural influence; to disclose preternaturally; to produce in, as by inspiration.
example: The speaker tried to inspire the audience with a calm but forceful appeal.

definition: Steadfast adherence to a strict moral or ethical code.
example: The speech returned to the idea of integrity as a guiding principle.

definition: The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
example: the justice of a description

definition: Money or property bequeathed to someone in a will.
example: The speech returned to the idea of legacy as a guiding principle.

definition: An instance of listening.
example: Give the motor a listen and tell me if it sounds off.
